design_philosophy.core_loop: Select pitch/batter action, execute swing/throw, progress through innings toward victory
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time decision making between pitcher and batter with licensed MLB authenticity
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Manage and play as an authentic MLB team with real players and stadiums
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Prioritizes licensed authenticity (first game with both MLB player AND team licenses) and accurate stadium recreation over arcade accessibility; positions itself as simulation-oriented baseball rather than arcade-style

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Team selection and lineup management provide strategic difficulty modulation; likely variable AI opponent strength

replayability_hooks: Multiple licensed MLB teams with different rosters and capabilities
Seasonal play structure with multiple games per season

world_building_techniques: Licensed authentic MLB team names and player rosters from 1993-1994 seasons
Accurate graphical recreation of real MLB stadiums
Professional play-by-play commentary by Jerry Coleman (actual San Diego Padres broadcaster)

faction_indicators: 30 licensed MLB teams representing real geographic and organizational factions
Team rivalries inherent to actual league structure
environmental_storytelling: Catcher's-eye perspective grounds player in realistic field positioning and spatial awareness
Stadium-specific architectural features influence gameplay strategy and ball physics

sources_used: wikipedia: World Series Baseball (1994 video game)
